title: diastema divaricatum
intro: Diastema divaricatum, also known as the Devil's Claw or Wood Spider Flower, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. The roots of the plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds, particularly harpagoside. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving arthritis and other joint pain, and aiding in digestive issues. Diastema divaricatum can be prepared as an herbal remedy in various forms, such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
